name: WFBE_NEURODEF_ARTYPOS_WEST
notes: ARTILLERY BATTERY (HEAVY, 4 AI) — WEST. Three M119 howitzers, EACH in its own earthen artillery revetment, spaced ~10 m apart so a counter-battery salvo or an air strike cannot bracket the whole battery in one impact (dispersion = survival for artillery). Every tube has clear sky (high-angle fire); revetments hide the guns from ground observation and eat shrapnel. Local security is a PAIR of rear .50 pickets angled outward so the battery's blind side and both rear corners are covered (a 3-gun line facing +Y is otherwise wide open behind). Ammo is dispersed one dump per gun behind the line, so a cook-off loses one gun, not three. Flank blast walls (HBarrier) stop enfilade; rear concealment nets sit BEHIND the guns only (never over the tubes); the rear centre is the crew's covered entrance.

objs.role: rear .50 picket (left) — covers rear-left blind corner
rear .50 picket (right) — covers rear-right blind corner
rear concealment (left) — behind the gun line only
rear concealment (right) — behind the gun line only
rear wire (left) — closes the flank approach
rear wire (right) — closes the flank approach
